name: Penedès 360 All Mountain: Montmell Loop
name_original: La Bisbal del Penedès - La Juncosa del Montmell  - Pla de Manlleu – circular por el Castillo de Selma
description: This 28.2-mile mountain biking loop in Penedès offers challenging terrain, 2937 feet of elevation gain, and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Bisbal Del Penedès offers a diverse range of routes across varied landscapes. The region features rolling hills, valleys, and mountain ranges like the Montmell-Marmellar Mountains, providing a mix of challenging ascents and descents. Extensive vineyards on the Penedès plain create picturesque backdrops, while forested areas contribute to varied terrain. This topography ensures a wide network of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around La Bisbal Del Penedès The most popular mountain bike trail is Penedès 360 All Mountain: Montmell Loop, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) moderate route that takes about 4 hours 40 minutes to complete, offering extensive views of the Montmell area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Access Trail to El Castellot – The Castellot loop from Baix Penedès, a difficult 25.7 miles (41.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain, including the historic Castellot. Local mountain bikers also love the Salto de Roca Trail – Gaià Reservoir loop from Masllorenç, a 27.9 miles (45.0 km) trail leading through the scenic Gaià Reservoir area, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around La Bisbal Del Penedès is defined by rolling hills, extensive vineyards, and forested mountain ranges. The network offers options for all ability levels, from easy rides through the plains to challenging routes in the higher elevations. The routes in La Bisbal Del Penedès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Bisbal Del Penedès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in La Bisbal Del Penedès?
A: La Bisbal Del Penedès offers a wide network of nearly 200 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides through the plains to challenging ascents in the higher elevations.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in La Bisbal Del Penedès?
A: Yes, there are over 30 easy mountain bike routes in the area. These often traverse the picturesque Penedès plain and its extensive vineyards, providing a gentler introduction to mountain biking while still offering scenic views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around La Bisbal Del Penedès?
A: The region features diverse terrain, including rolling hills, valleys, and mountain ranges like the Montmell-Marmellar Mountains. You'll encounter a mix of single tracks, technical trails, and paved rural roads, often with challenging ascents and enjoyable downhill sections. The Penedès plain also offers routes through vineyards.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are nearly 40 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Access Trail to El Castellot – The Castellot loop from Baix Penedès&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain and varied terrain over 25.7 miles (41.4 km).

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in La Bisbal Del Penedès?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958900506/penedes-360-all-mountain-montmell-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 All Mountain: Montmell Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route covering 28.3 miles (45.5 km) with extensive views of the Montmell area.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The area is rich in natural and historical sights. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle and the Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views. Other natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/477075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola (Highest Point of Tarragonès)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Dous Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. You may also spot unique dry stone shanties, recognized by UNESCO, adding a cultural element to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Bisbal Del Penedès?
A: Thanks to a mild microclimate protected by the Garraf hills and Montserrat mountains, La Bisbal Del Penedès is suitable for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures, but winter can also be enjoyable with proper gear.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer great viewpoints?
A: Many routes in the region provide excellent panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958900506/penedes-360-all-mountain-montmell-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penedès 360 All Mountain: Montmell Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views of the Montmell area. Additionally, elevated points like Coll de les Ventoses, a popular climb, provide rewarding vistas of the Penedès plain and the Mediterranean Sea. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5733267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cal Bladet Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in La Bisbal Del Penedès?
A: The mountain bike trails in La Bisbal Del Penedès are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling hills and vineyards to challenging mountain ranges, and the well-maintained network of trails that cater to all abilities.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers routes suitable for families, particularly the easier trails that traverse the flatter Penedès plain and its vineyards. These routes provide a less strenuous experience, allowing families to enjoy the scenic backdrop together.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific parking locations vary by trail, many routes around La Bisbal Del Penedès start from or pass through towns and villages where public parking is typically available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for detailed parking information.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: The Penedès region has public transport options, including bus and train services, connecting various towns. While some trailheads might be accessible via public transport, it's recommended to plan your route in advance and check local transport schedules and bike carriage policies to ensure a smooth journey to your starting point.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Penedès 360 All Mountain: Montmell Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is considered difficult and demanding. It requires a good level of fitness and advanced riding skills due to its steep climbs, technical sections with rocks, roots, and steps. Some portions may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, featuring dirt roads, farm tracks, and paths. You'll encounter steep mountain slopes with technical sections, including stony paths, especially on the way to Selma Castle. Surfaces range from natural ground to some paved and asphalt sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Montmell Loop?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features. You'll pass by L'Església Nova del Montmell, enjoy a View of Pico del Montmell, and explore the ruins of Selma Castle. Another highlight is the Chapel of Sant Miquel del Pla de Manlleu. Beyond these, you'll also find the remains of the medieval fortress Castellot and the Creu del Montmell, a significant high point with extensive views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: You can ride this route almost year-round. However, spring and autumn are ideal due to milder temperatures, making the demanding climbs more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is the Penedès 360 All Mountain: Montmell Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, it's a challenging mountain biking route with technical sections and varied terrain. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are fit enough for the distance and elevation, and always keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Montmell Loop?
faqs.answer: The tour starts near La Bisbal del Penedès. Specific parking information isn't detailed, but generally, you can look for parking options in or around the starting villages like La Bisbal del Penedès or La Juncosa del Montmell. Always check local signage for parking regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride the Penedès 360 All Mountain: Montmell Loop. It's an open route through public and rural lands.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The Montmell Loop offers breathtaking 360-degree natural viewpoints. You'll see expansive vineyard landscapes of the Penedès, charming villages, forests, and often the majestic Montserrat mountain on the horizon. The scenery transitions from mountains to glimpses of the sea.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride parts of the Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies), Camí Ral de Vilafranca a Montblanc, and La Bisbal del Penedés-Sant Magí de Bufaganya, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through small villages like La Juncosa del Montmell and Pla de Manlleu. While specific amenities aren't listed, these villages may offer basic services, cafes, or rural accommodation options. It's advisable to check in advance, especially for food and water supplies.

faqs.question: What should I pack for mountain biking the Montmell Loop?
faqs.answer: Given the route's demanding nature, you should pack plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate mountain biking gear (helmet, gloves, padded shorts), sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A fully charged phone with the komoot app for navigation is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Chapel of Sant Miquel del Pla de Manlleu
